stories.summary: UN human rights chief Volker Turk issued the warning over growing fears of an imminent RSF assault.

stories.headline: Israel approves plan to establish 13 new settlements in occupied West Bank

stories.headline: Russia’s triumphant tone shifts as Ukraine deploys ‘asymmetrical tactics’
stories.summary: Putin wobbles as Russia's top brass stays mum on Kyiv's long-range strikes, which analysts say look set to continue.

stories.headline: EU lawmaker investigating surveillance hacked by Israeli spyware, report says

stories.headline: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.

stories.headline: At least 32 dead after overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in Pakistan
stories.summary: The bus was travelling from Quetta to Peshawar, when it crashed in the Dana Sar mountain range.

stories.headline: Huge Snicko VAR call prolongs Ronaldo's last dance but ends Modric's
stories.summary: Veteran forward Cristiano Ronaldo gets another day on the World Cup stage after Croatia are denied a late equaliser by a huge VAR call.

stories.headline: At the L.A. Warehouse Fire, First It Was the Smoke. Now It’s the Smell.
stories.summary: Rotting food — millions of pounds of it — remains in an L.A. cold-storage facility damaged in a fire. Residents say the cleanup hasn’t been fast enough.

stories.headline: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
stories.summary: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipientAs Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister.Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. stories.headline: Moldova’s prime minister quits, triggering government’s resignation
stories.summary: Alexandru Munteanu's resignation came as pressure has intensified over alleged corruption in a state-owned company.

stories.headline: Moldovan prime minister Alexandru Munteanu resigns after eight months in office

stories.headline: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills nine, Syrian state media say
stories.summary: There was no immediate claim for the bombing at a cafe close to the Palace of Justice, a major government building.

stories.summary: Copying the investments made by Trump and other political elites is one potential tactic but it carries risks

stories.headline: Gold Heads for First Weekly Gain Since May on Easing Fed Outlook
stories.summary: Gold headed for its first weekly gain since May as traders dialed down their expectations of Federal Reserve rate hikes.

stories.headline: Copper Tracks Dollar Swings as Traders Weigh US Rate Outlook
stories.summary: Copper edged higher, tracking small swings in the US dollar as investors continue to assess the outlook for US monetary policy.

stories.headline: Carlyle Hires Banks for $400 Million India IPO of RCM Business
stories.summary: Carlyle Group Inc. has hired JM Financial Ltd. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to advise on a potential initial public offering of its India-based healthcare revenue cycle management business, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Pubs to Get Short-Term World Cup Boost
stories.summary: UK pubs will be allowed to stay open for Monday’s early morning match, but the overall benefit of the World Cup will likely be short-lived. Bloomberg's Sam Unsed discusses on "The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Russia Hikes June Subsidies to Refiners as Fuel Shortages Bite
stories.summary: Russia’s subsidy payouts to oil refiners that supply domestic markets jumped more than six-fold in June from a year earlier, as Moscow sought to avoid shortages by keeping more fuel at home.

stories.headline: Adani Group Secures $15 Billion Capital This Week as US Legal Clouds Clear
stories.summary: In one week, billionaire Gautam Adani has announced nearly $15 billion in investment commitments across its ports, mining and flagship businesses.

stories.headline: CVC Plans €1.2 Billion of High-Yield Bonds to Fund Irca Buyout
stories.summary: CVC Capital Partners Plc is set to finance its buyout of Italian food-ingredients maker Irca SpA with €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) of high-yield bonds, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: China’s State Iron Ore Buyer Extends Restrictions on Fortescue
stories.summary: China’s state-backed iron ore buyer has expanded its curbs on Fortescue Ltd.’s iron ore to include new purchases of its Super Special Fines product and not just portside inventories, escalating its dispute with the Australian miner.

stories.headline: Indonesia’s Danantara Wealth Fund Sees Senior Management Shakeup
stories.summary: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara is reshuffling its management team with a series of new hires, according to people familiar with the matter, as it expands its role under President Prabowo Subianto’s government.

stories.headline: Trump Doubles Down on Fed Changes, Burnham Risks Starmer Mistakes  | The Opening Trade 7/2/2026
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump and his allies are renewing a push to reshape the Federal Reserve after the Supreme Court this week blocked an effort to fire Governor Lisa Cook.

stories.headline: ECB Is in ‘Good Position’ After June Rate Hike, Moulin Says
stories.summary: The European Central Bank is in a comfortable situation after last month’s interest-rate increase as the falling oil price eases price pressures in the euro zone, Governing Council member Emmanuel Moulin said.

stories.headline: Taiwanese Brokerage Yuanta Taps Banks for $1.3 Billion of Loans
stories.summary: Yuanta Securities Co., Taiwan’s largest brokerage, and its subsidiary, are seeking up to NT$42 billion ($1.3 billion) through two syndicated loans, according to people familiar with the matter, a fundraising that highlights surging demand for financial services amid the stock boom.

stories.headline: An AIDS-free generation is within reach, but not guaranteed

stories.headline: Killers of British couple in South Africa sentenced to life
stories.summary: The pair had been collecting rare plants and seeds before their bodies were found days later in a river.

stories.headline: China says pilot crashed small plane into skyscraper for 'personal reasons'

stories.summary: David Hearn, a former canoeist, is accused of vandalizing the algae-challenged pool, which President Trump has said he would repair and beautify with fresh sealant. But the algae returned and the paint peeled.

stories.headline: European Stocks Head for Best Week Since May: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks rose as the latest round of jitters about the artificial-intelligence trade subsided, with Europe’s benchmark adding to an all-time high. The dollar touched a two-week low while gold extended gains.

stories.headline: MAGA Base Stays Quiet After Trump Reports Billions in Personal Gains

stories.summary: The expansion of an investigation into Fulton County’s election office reflects President Trump’s desire to prove his baseless claims that the 2020 election in Georgia was rigged.

stories.headline: Can China target critics abroad with its new 'ethnic unity' law?
stories.summary: Rights groups fear the new law gives Beijing legal authority to go after people beyond its borders.

stories.headline: US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal

stories.headline: Greek politician's mother dies of wounds after arson attack

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas

stories.summary: Teams armed with a range of techniques continue to dig through the rubble in Venezuela.

stories.headline: Fourteen killed in Saudi Arabia helicopter crash
stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire

stories.headline: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline

stories.headline: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.headline: Trump accuses Iran of ceasefire breach after Strait of Hormuz attack

stories.headline: Rainbow flags await Egypt and Iran at awkward Pride Match
